prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register-unit tank_alpha)
(register-unit dps_alpha)
(register-unit coordinator_lead)
(register-unit coordinator_support)
(assign-anchor-to-unit tank_alpha aggro_anchor_a)
(assign-anchor-to-unit dps_alpha aggro_anchor_b)
(assign-anchor-to-unit coordinator_lead aggro_anchor_c)
(lock-target-for-unit tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(confirm-role-lock tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(assign-support-to-unit tank_alpha support_agent_a)
(reserve-melee-slot tank_alpha melee_slot_a enemy_target_a)
(confirm-melee-slot-with-support tank_alpha melee_slot_a support_agent_a)
(release-support-from-unit tank_alpha support_agent_a)
(release-target-from-unit tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(lock-target-for-unit dps_alpha enemy_target_a)
(confirm-role-lock dps_alpha enemy_target_a)
(assign-support-to-unit dps_alpha support_agent_a)
(reserve-ranged-slot dps_alpha ranged_slot_a enemy_target_a)
(confirm-ranged-slot-with-support dps_alpha ranged_slot_a support_agent_a)
(release-support-from-unit dps_alpha support_agent_a)
(release-target-from-unit dps_alpha enemy_target_a)
(create-engagement-marker-standard tank_alpha dps_alpha melee_slot_a ranged_slot_a engagement_marker_a)
(lock-target-for-unit tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(finalize-engagement-marker engagement_marker_a tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(mark-tank-ready-for-engagement tank_alpha engagement_marker_a)
(mark-dps-ready-for-engagement dps_alpha engagement_marker_a)
(bind-taunt-charge-to-unit tank_alpha taunt_charge_a enemy_target_a)
(commit-tank-engagement tank_alpha aggro_anchor_a taunt_charge_a)
(release-target-from-unit tank_alpha enemy_target_a)
(lock-target-for-unit coordinator_lead enemy_target_a)
(confirm-role-lock coordinator_lead enemy_target_a)
(allocate-mechanic-to-marker coordinator_lead mechanic_objective_a engagement_marker_a)
(coordinator-prepare-stage-one coordinator_lead mechanic_objective_a engagement_marker_a enemy_target_a)
(attach-device-to-coordinator coordinator_lead special_device_a)
(assign-support-to-unit coordinator_lead support_agent_a)
(coordinator-lock-device-for-marker coordinator_lead special_device_a support_agent_a mechanic_objective_a engagement_marker_a)
(attach-timed-trigger-to-coordinator coordinator_lead timed_trigger_a)
(coordinator-arm-trigger coordinator_lead timed_trigger_a mechanic_objective_a engagement_marker_a)
(finalize-coordinator-preparation-basic coordinator_lead)
(bind-taunt-charge-to-unit coordinator_lead taunt_charge_a enemy_target_a)
(commit-coordinator-engagement coordinator_lead aggro_anchor_c taunt_charge_a)
(release-target-from-unit coordinator_lead enemy_target_a)
(assign-anchor-to-unit coordinator_support aggro_anchor_a)
(lock-target-for-unit coordinator_support enemy_target_a)
(confirm-role-lock coordinator_support enemy_target_a)
(allocate-mechanic-to-marker coordinator_support mechanic_objective_b engagement_marker_a)
(coordinator-prepare-stage-one coordinator_support mechanic_objective_b engagement_marker_a enemy_target_a)
(detach-device-from-coordinator coordinator_lead special_device_a)
(attach-device-to-coordinator coordinator_support special_device_a)
(assign-support-to-unit coordinator_support support_agent_a)
(coordinator-lock-device-for-marker coordinator_support special_device_a support_agent_a mechanic_objective_b engagement_marker_a)
(detach-timed-trigger-from-coordinator coordinator_lead timed_trigger_a)
(attach-timed-trigger-to-coordinator coordinator_support timed_trigger_a)
(coordinator-arm-trigger coordinator_support timed_trigger_a mechanic_objective_b engagement_marker_a)
(finalize-coordinator-preparation-basic coordinator_support)
(bind-taunt-charge-to-unit coordinator_support taunt_charge_a enemy_target_a)
(commit-coordinator-engagement coordinator_support aggro_anchor_a taunt_charge_a)
(release-target-from-unit coordinator_support enemy_target_a)
(lock-target-for-unit dps_alpha enemy_target_a)
(bind-taunt-charge-to-unit dps_alpha taunt_charge_a enemy_target_a)
(commit-dps-engagement dps_alpha aggro_anchor_b taunt_charge_a)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for aggro management coordination. I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve it, each on its own line in the form (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Identifying Problem Details  
- The task involves solving a planning problem in PDDL related to aggro management coordination.  
- The goal is to output a sequence of ground actions, each on its own line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).

## Examining Initial Setup  
- The system includes various available resources like anchors (a, b, c), enemy_target_a, support_agent_a, and consumables, plus specific slots and markers.  
- Initial assignments show tank_alpha linked to melee_slot_a and dps_alpha to ranged_slot_a, with coordinators tied to tank, dps, and a marker.  

## Checking Resource Availability  
- Priority_flag_a is available, but priority_flag_b is assigned to support despite not being initially available, suggesting pre-assignment.  
- Only one enemy_target_a is available, which multiple units may need to share for target-locking, potentially requiring release and reuse.  

## Analyzing Engagement Requirements  
- To commit engagement, units like tank_alpha and dps_alpha need taunt_ready, unit_bound_to_aggro_anchor, and taunt_bound_to_unit, eventually setting engagement_committed.  
- Ready_for_engagement for tanks involves marking tank_ready, confirming slots, and activating/finalizing markers, indicating multiple preparatory steps.  

## Planning Initial Actions  
- All units (tank_alpha, dps_alpha, coordinator_lead, coordinator_support) need registration first, as initial state lacks unit_registered, using register_unit for each.  
- After registration, assigning anchors and locking targets will likely follow, considering limited resources like the single enemy_target_a.

## Exploring Engagement Paths  
- Coordinators can achieve ready_for_engagement through a staged approach (stages 1,2,3) or a priority path using flags.  
- The staged path involves preparing stages with conditions like role_locked, target_locked, and mechanic allocations, ending with finalize_basic.  

## Checking Priority Path Details  
- The priority path requires assigning a priority flag (only a available initially) and activating it with support, leading to priority_finalized.  
- It offers an alternative to stages, finalizing with triggers and marking ready_for_engagement, but relies on limited flag availability.  

## Analyzing Support Resource Management  
- Support can be assigned to units for slot confirmation but requires release_support_from_unit to reuse, as only one support (support_agent_a) is available.  
- This allows sequential assignment to tank and dps, confirming slots, but complicates coordination with limited resources.  

## Planning Role Locking Steps  
- Role locking for tanks and dps involves registration, anchor assignment, target locking, and role confirmation, ensuring readiness.  
- Markers may require tank_ready, dps_ready, and reserved slots, leveraging initial bindings for standard creation.

## Preparing DPS for Engagement  
- To prepare dps_alpha, need to lock enemy_target_a, currently locked by tank_alpha, so release target from tank_alpha first.  
- After releasing, register dps_alpha, assign anchorZ, lock enemy_target_a, and confirm role_lock, ensuring dps_alpha is ready.  

## Managing Support Assignments  
- Assign support_agent_a to dps_alpha after releasing it from tank_alpha, then reserve and confirm ranged slot, marking dps_ready.  
- Release support_agent_a from dps_alpha after slot confirmation to make it available for reuse, ensuring resource flexibility.  

## Finalizing Engagement Marker  
- Create standard engagement marker with tank_alpha and dps_alpha, linking melee_slot_a and ranged_slot_a, then finalize it with target_locked tank_alpha.  
- Lock enemy_target_a for tank_alpha again after release, ensuring finalize_engagement_marker can proceed, marking both tank and dps ready_for_engagement.  

## Addressing Anchor Limitations  
- Only three anchors (anchor_a, b, c) are available for four units, requiring commitment of some units to release anchors for others.  
- Plan to prepare tank_alpha, dps_alpha, and coordinators without committing yet, then commit to free anchors, ensuring all units eventually engage.

## Preparing Tank for Engagement  
- Lock enemy_target_a for tank_alpha to begin preparation, ensuring target availability.  
- Confirm role_lock for tank_alpha, assign support_agent_a, and reserve melee_slot_a, marking tank_ready after slot confirmation.  

## Managing Support Availability  
- Release support_agent_a from tank_alpha after slot confirmation, making it available for dps_alpha.  
- Release enemy_target_a from tank_alpha before dps_alpha can lock it, ensuring sequential target management.  

## Preparing DPS for Engagement  
- Lock enemy_target_a for dps_alpha after releasing it from tank_alpha, then confirm role_lock and assign support_agent_a.  
- Reserve ranged_slot_a, confirm with support, and mark dps_ready, releasing support and target afterward for reuse.  

## Creating Engagement Marker  
- Create standard engagement marker linking tank_alpha, dps_alpha, melee_slot_a, and ranged_slot_a, activating it for use.  
- Finalize the marker with tank_alpha target-locked to enemy_target_a, then mark both tank and dps ready_for_engagement.  

## Addressing Resource Constraints  
- Anchors (a, b, c) are all assigned, limiting coordinator_support preparation due to anchor shortage.  
- Consider committing tank_alpha first by binding taunt_charge_a, releasing anchor_a and taunt for reuse in subsequent steps.

## Addressing Trigger Limitations  
- Only one timed_trigger_a is available, posing a problem for preparing two coordinators for stage3, which requires an assigned trigger.  
- Found that detach_timed_trigger_from_coordinator can free the trigger after stage3 is set, allowing reuse for the next coordinator.  

## Managing Stage Preparation  
- All paths to ready_for_engagement (finalize_preparation_*) require stage3_ready, assembly, or priority_finalized, all needing a trigger or stage3.  
- After arm_trigger sets stage3_ready, the trigger remains assigned but can be detached afterward, ensuring availability for subsequent steps.  

## Exploring Boosting Mechanics  
- Boosting melee slots, like with boost_melee_slot_with_consumable, requires the tank not be tank_ready, but ours is already confirmed.  
- Consider boosting before confirming support, but this complicates the current marker setup with two coordinators.  

## Coordinating Multiple Units  
- Noted that two coordinators can allocate mechanics to the same tank_dps marker, potentially simplifying resource sharing.  
- Plan to have one coordinator use priority with trigger, then detach and pass to the next, optimizing limited resources.

## Reviewing Action Sequence  
- The sequence shows the marker was created and finalized before committing the tank, with the tank target locked to the enemy at step 22.  
- After committing the tank at step 27, the target was released at step 28, then later locked for the lead at step 29, ensuring smooth transitions.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
